mechanism: Quassinoid-rich bitter constituents appear to stimulate bitter-taste receptor pathways that increase digestive secretions; antiparasitic activity is supported mainly by preclinical data.
contraindications: avoid during pregnancy because concentrated bitter principles may increase uterine activity risk
avoid in active peptic ulcer or severe gastritis
interactions: may increase gastrointestinal irritation when combined with NSAIDs or other gastric irritants
use caution with other strongly bitter digestive stimulants
safetyNotes: Moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician; Avoid combining with other stimulants.
description: Quassia amara is a medicinal herb traditionally used for bitter tonic and digestive stimulant.
preparation: Wood chips infused or decocted; intensely bitter, traditionally used for digestive and antiparasitic purposes
